place: Averill Park CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Averill Park got its name in 1880 from the Averill family, then added 'Park' in 1882 to boost summer‑resort appeal.
19th‑century watermills on the Wynants Kill turned Averill Park into a wool and cotton textile hub before a 1891 flood wiped out the mills.
The Faith Knitting Company, founded in 1897, revived the town’s mills by rehiring local workers.
A planned trolley line from Troy to Averill Park never reached completion, but an electric trolley arrived in 1895, linking the town to regional rail.
Arts Letters & Numbers, a nonprofit arts center on Burden Lake Road, occupies the former Sand Lake Cotton Factory, once owned by Arnolds, Hunt & Co.
The Sand Lake Baptist Church, standing in Averill Park, earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places in 2004.
